player-mage.src: pmndrs/market-assets (Pirate Captain) -- hooded/cloaked blocky humanoid used as a stand-in for a robed spellcaster; no CC0 wizard/mage model was found in any reachable source
player-mage.author: Kenney
player-mage.note: not a literal wizard model -- closest style-appropriate CC0 humanoid found; consider a purple/blue recolor or a small staff prop to sell the mage read

enemy-rusher.src: pmndrs/market-assets (Zombie, variant 1) -- shipped in a custom arms-down idle pose (see note)
enemy-rusher.author: Kenney
enemy-rusher.note: source asset is a skinned rig with ZERO baked animation clips (bind pose is a T-pose). Skin/joints were baked out via full linear-blend skinning into a static arms-down pose (shoulders rotated ~75deg) so it doesn't render as a scarecrow T-pose; shipped mesh has no skin/animations.

enemy-swarmer.src: pmndrs/market-assets (Zombie, variant 2) -- same rig family/pose treatment as enemy-rusher, different texture/palette (skull tee, backpack)
enemy-swarmer.author: Kenney
enemy-swarmer.note: no CC0 tiny non-humanoid critter (slime/bee/spider/bat) was found in any reachable source; this reuses the rusher's rig with a different skin, scaled down by the integrator. A true tiny-critter role remains unfilled -- see report. Same static arms-down repose as enemy-rusher; no skin/animations shipped.

enemy-caster.src: pmndrs/market-assets (Female Cyborg) -- reposed arms-down idle, tech/arcane-construct look substituted for skeleton/ghost/cultist
enemy-caster.author: Kenney
enemy-caster.note: same T-pose-bind / no-clips situation as enemy-rusher, fixed the same way (baked static arms-down pose, no skin/animations shipped).

enemy-brute.src: pmndrs/market-assets (Pirate Officer) -- bulky blocky-body silhouette (tricorn hat, coat) used as a heavy/brute stand-in
enemy-brute.author: Kenney

enemy-boss.src: pmndrs/market-assets (Pirate Crew) -- same blocky body family as enemy-brute with a different cap/color, per the brief's allowance for the boss to be 'a clearly-different variant of brute'; intended to be scaled up substantially by the integrator
enemy-boss.author: Kenney

table.author: saravieira
table.note: geometry only -- the source asset's large photo-real CC0Textures/Wood027 JPEG material (normal+color+roughness, ~4MB) was intentionally NOT embedded to respect the low-poly flat-shaded style rule and the size budget; replaced with a flat baseColorFactor sampled from the texture's mean color. Native footprint is a long picnic/banquet table (3.7x1.4), not a small round table -- scale down if a smaller table is wanted.

ground-grass.author: OpenGameArt.org contributor (via three.js terrain/readme.txt); repackaged by three.js authors

ground-lava.license: MIT (repo-level; no per-asset readme.txt exists for this file, unlike terrain/ and cube/*)
ground-lava.author: three.js authors

ground-snow.author: ambientCG (ambientcg.com) -- all ambientCG materials are published CC0 1.0 Universal; the three.js repo mirrors it verbatim in a folder literally named 'ambientcg'

ground-stone.author: three.js authors
ground-stone.note: a brick-wall photo, not a natural rock/stone scan -- no CC0/CC-BY natural-rock ground texture was found in any reachable source. Reads as a flagstone/cobblestone floor after grayscale+tiling. Original crop had a visible seam at the brick coursing; fixed with an offset+heal (roll by half + localized blur at the new center seam).

ground-void.author: three.js authors
ground-void.note: abstract regular triangle-grid pattern, used for the Void theme's ground; not a natural material so seamlessness is inherent (a regular repeating grid).
